code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    breakout_multiplier = params['breakout_multiplier']
    ma_window = params['ma_window']
    
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window).average_true_range()
    df['ma'] = df['close'].rolling(window=ma_window).mean()
    
    df['upper_band'] = df['ma'] + (df['atr'] * breakout_multiplier)
    df['lower_band'] = df['ma'] - (df['atr'] * breakout_multiplier)
    
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype='int')
    signals[(df['close'] > df['upper_band'].shift(1)) & (df['close'] > df['ma'])] = 1
    signals[(df['close'] < df['lower_band'].shift(1)) & (df['close'] < df['ma'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/JPY exhibits volatility clustering where breakouts from average true range bands around a moving average signal continuation of the trend.
